notes: Architectural state is intentionally minimal here: this repo treats the compiled catalog as the primary machine-readable source.
v0.56 keeps the v0.2 architectural baseline and promotes the canonical shader-kernel and rendering contracts.
Contradictory raw legacy fragments are archived history only and are reserved/illegal in canonical v0.56.
v0.56.4 keeps PRED with plen[1:0] plus p0..p3 mask bits.
v0.56.4 keeps the 64-logical-tile model with dynamic capacity descriptors.

kernel_exec_mask.inactive_lane_result_for_mask_producer: inactive lanes clear their destination bit to 0
kernel_exec_mask.inactive_lane_rule: inactive lanes follow the selected architectural merge/zero policy
kernel_exec_mask.name: p
kernel_exec_mask.packed_lane_rule: packed convert/compare operations update plen and p0..p3 according to the number of logical elements carried in each lane

group_execution_mode.MPAR: groups/lanes may execute in parallel but must preserve architectural ordering guarantees
group_execution_mode.MSEQ: groups/lanes retire in lexicographic LC order

simt_group_model.scalar_uniform_lane: each group has one scalar-uniform lane context; scalar body instructions execute once per group replay

scalar_imports.binding_rule: allocated from repeated B.IOR descriptors in header order, then RegSrc0/RegSrc1/RegSrc2 within each descriptor

vector_body_mixed_execution.replay: body executes as a structured instruction stream with one scalar-uniform control context per group

vector_instruction_operand_contract.any_operand_rule: if any operand names vt/vu/vm/vn the instruction executes as v.*; otherwise it executes as l.*
vector_instruction_operand_contract.cross_domain_write_rule: v.* may not write scalar/group-domain destinations except through explicitly defined reduction or mask-producing instructions
vector_instruction_operand_contract.exception: zero literal allowed where encoding permits
vector_instruction_operand_contract.scalar_inputs: must be imported via B.IOR and consumed as ri0..ri11 or other defined scalar/group operand classes
vector_instruction_operand_contract.vector_broadcast_rule: scalar/group-domain source operands broadcast to all active lanes when consumed by v.*

compiler_staged_lowering.identity_requirement: staged reduction lowering requires identity initializer for the selected reduction operation
compiler_staged_lowering.liveout_bridge: for strict bring-up, compiler may materialize reduction live-out through `V.RD*` followed by a bridged scratch store (`V.SW.BRG` via ri* binding)

vector_reduction_contract.mp_ar_compiler_rule: compiler emits MPAR reduction only when dependence-safe (associative/commutative or explicit programmer parallel hint)
vector_reduction_contract.scope: reduce lane values to a scalar destination
